name: Server-Sent Events stream reachable by anonymous callers
description: GETs a Server-Sent Events endpoint with no credentials. When the server answers with a text/event-stream response and starts pushing events (data: frames) to an unauthenticated caller, any client that can reach the URL receives the server-push feed — order updates, notifications, presence, audit events, whatever the stream carries. SSE streams are easy to ship without auth because they look like a plain GET; the misconfiguration is that the same authorization gating the REST endpoints isn't applied to the event stream. The predicate looks for both the event-stream content type and a data: frame so a normal HTML/JSON 200 doesn't trip it.

vulnerability.remediation: Apply the same authorization policy to the SSE endpoint that protects the underlying data. ASP.NET Core: annotate the streaming endpoint with `[Authorize]` (or `.RequireAuthorization()`), validate the token before writing the first event, and scope the events to what the authenticated caller may see (never broadcast every tenant's events on one stream). Verify with `curl -k -N https://host/events` — a gated stream returns 401 before any `data:` frame. If the feed is intentionally public, document it and ensure it carries no per-user / sensitive data.
